result.meeting_context: This is the first meeting of 2023 for the Mountain View Human Relations Commission (HRC), welcoming three new commissioners and conducting elections for Chair and Vice Chair positions. The meeting covers work plan updates, subcommittee assignments, and transitions from virtual to in-person meetings.

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Commissioner Raigstraw resigned from HRC effective immediately and will not be joining the meeting.
points.source: Laurel James : I received a notice from Commissioner Raigstraw this evening that she'll be resigning from the HRC effective immediately

points.speaker: Ida Rose Sylvester
points.text: Three new HRC members joined: Jocelyn, Molly and Eric Poicon, representing the greatest number of new members at a single meeting.
points.source: Ida Rose Sylvester : it's also extremely exciting to welcome our new members Jocelyn, Molly and Eric Poicon. Today I believe the greatest number of new HRC members we've ever sat at a single meeting

points.speaker: Ida Rose Sylvester
points.text: This is theoretically the last virtual meeting after three years of virtual meetings.
points.source: Ida Rose Sylvester : what is our last virtual meeting unless something has changed and it's like, Oh yeah, you never know. But it theoretically our last virtual meeting after three years

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Next HRC meeting will be in person at City Hall on February 2nd at 6:30 PM in the Plaza conference room.
points.source: Laurel James : the next HRC meeting will not be virtual. It will be in person at City Hall on, I believe it's Thursday, February the 2nd at 6:30 PM

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Housing and Security storytelling project event is scheduled for May 25th, 2023 at Mountain View Community Center in the Redwood room.
points.source: Laurel James : The event related to this Housing and Security Project Storytelling project is currently scheduled for May 25th, 2023 and it will be held at the Mountain View Community Center in the Redwood room

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Mental Health subcommittee event is tentatively scheduled for late June 2023, though no specific date has been selected.
points.source: Laurel James : the event is tentatively scheduled for late June 2023, although no date has been selected yet

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Spring Family Parade participation is upcoming in April, with date to be announced when available.
points.source: Laurel James : one of the ongoing or regular items that is annual in the Work Plan Item G is participating in the annual Spring Family Parade. That event will be upcoming in April

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Housing and Security Subcommittee currently has Vice Chair Sylvester and Commissioner Webb with one opening available.
points.source: Laurel James : The subcommittee has met several times and consists Vice Chair Sylvester as well as Commissioner. I believe Commissioner Annette Lynn, who was previously the Chair of the HRC was on that Commission that Excuse me subcommittee as well along with Commissioner Webb

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Bystander Intervention Training subcommittee currently has only Commissioner Webb, as other two members are no longer on HRC.
points.source: Laurel James : Currently, I believe the only member of that subcommittee is Commissioner Webb, as the other two members of that subcommittee are no longer in the HRC

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Final subcommittee assignments: Bystander Intervention (Webb, Baird, Poicon), Mental Health (Sylvester, Nwokido, Smith), Housing and Security (Sylvester, Webb, Poicon).
points.source: Laurel James : our Bystander Intervention Subcommittee would be comprised of Commissioners Webb, Baird and Poicon, and the Mental Health Subcommittee would include yourself, Vice Chair Sylvester, Commissioner Wilkidu, and Commissioner Smith. And then Housing and Security would be comprised of you, Vice Chair Sylvester, Commissioner Webb and Commissioner Poicon

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Ida Rose Sylvester was unanimously elected as HRC Chair for 2023.
points.source: Laurel James : So we'll be voting on whether or not to elect her as chair of the HRC

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: Nirvana Nwokido was unanimously elected as HRC Vice Chair for 2023.
points.source: Laurel James : for the election of the Vice Chair, we will be voting on the nomination of Commissioner Volkito

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: City Clerk's Office sent invitations for Brown Act Refresher training for mid-January, which is critical for maintaining public trust and compliance.
points.source: Laurel James : This is a reminder that the City Clerk's Office has sent you invitation for Brown Act Refresher training for mid January, so please don't forget to attend. It's critical for maintaining the public trust

points.speaker: Laurel James
points.text: New commissioners will receive Form 700 emails from Netfile platform, which is legitimate city communication requesting personal financial information.
points.source: Laurel James : for those of you who have not yet filed your Form 700 because you are new commissioners, you will receive an e-mail from a platform called Netfile that is from the city. That is not spam

points.speaker: Xavier Webb
points.text: Xavier Webb expressed excitement about new commissioners joining and praised their enthusiasm for making community impact.
points.source: Xavier Webb : I'm very, very excited to have our new commissioners on board. You all seem very enthusiastic and that's what keeps this Commission alive is enthusiastic individuals who want to make a difference

points.speaker: Ida Rose Sylvester
points.text: Chair Sylvester thanked commissioners and expressed anticipation for working together, noting excitement about chairing in-person meetings.
points.source: Ida Rose Sylvester : Thank you all for intrusting in me to keep sitting in this well, actually no longer in this seat. It will be an honor to actually chair in person

decisions.text: All commissioners must attend Brown Act Refresher training scheduled for mid-January.
decisions.source: Laurel James : This is a reminder that the City Clerk's Office has sent you invitation for Brown Act Refresher training for mid January, so please don't forget to attend

decisions.text: New commissioners must complete Form 700 filing when they receive emails from Netfile platform.
decisions.source: Laurel James : for those of you who have not yet filed your Form 700 because you are new commissioners, you will receive an e-mail from a platform called Netfile

decisions.text: Next meeting will be held in person at City Hall Plaza conference room on February 2nd, 2023 at 6:30 PM.
decisions.source: Laurel James : the next HRC meeting will not be virtual. It will be in person at City Hall on, I believe it's Thursday, February the 2nd at 6:30 PM
